title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation
description: Anthropic closed a $65 billion Series H round led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, pushing its post-money valuation to $965 billion and eclipsing rival OpenAI for the first time. The company reports a $47 billion annual revenue run rate and $10 billion in revenue last year. Proceeds will fund safety and interpretability research, expanded compute capacity, and product and partnership scaling.

title: Scaling Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber
description: OpenAI introduced GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber, its most capable models for cybersecurity tasks, available through its Trusted Access for Cyber program. The models are designed for permissive use by vetted security professionals, with Advanced Account Security becoming mandatory for program members starting June 1, 2026. The release marks OpenAI's deepest push yet into the professional cybersecurity market.

title: OpenAI launches the OpenAI Deployment Company to help businesses build around intelligence
description: OpenAI announced the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new entity that will partner directly with enterprises to design and execute AI deployments around its models. The move is a significant expansion beyond API access, positioning OpenAI as a hands-on implementation partner alongside its existing consulting push. It follows OpenAI's earlier hire of enterprise-focused consultants and signals a shift toward capturing more of the enterprise services market.

title: Meta Unveils Subscriptions for AI While OpenAI, xAI Target Meta's Ad Business
description: Meta announced a paid subscription tier for its AI chatbot, marking the company's first move to monetize its consumer AI products directly rather than relying solely on advertising. The announcement came as rivals OpenAI and xAI stepped up competition for Meta's core ad business, adding pressure across both revenue streams. The subscription model mirrors offerings from ChatGPT and Claude, signaling a broader industry convergence on the freemium AI assistant model.

title: Teaching Claude why
description: Anthropic published a research post detailing its approach to teaching Claude not just what behaviors are expected but the underlying reasoning and values behind them, aiming to build models that can generalize to novel situations. The work is part of Anthropic's broader interpretability and alignment research agenda, seeking to make Claude's decision-making more robust and principled rather than pattern-matched. The post offers transparency into how Anthropic's safety training philosophy translates into model development practice.

title: How long is Anthropic's lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary
description: TechCrunch reported that Anthropic's multi-billion-dollar compute agreement with SpaceX for access to the Colossus 1 data center contains a 180-day term with a 90-day mutual cancellation clause, raising questions about the deal's long-term stability despite its headline-grabbing $45 billion price tag. Differing interpretations of the contract terms led to public confusion about whether Anthropic has secured multi-year compute or a shorter-term arrangement. The piece highlights how compute agreements have become a key battleground for AI labs seeking to signal strength to investors and customers.
